confirmation.detail: inbox envelope env_31d1d128-c453-4a20-a1bf-d70484b4ddc4 read back as received

missing_tools: Canonical operator-surface agent fork/start command that composes identity admission, handoff packet generation, terminal profile/materialization, process launch, runtime binding, adoption evidence, and initial prompt delivery.
Structured CLI launch contract for passing a handoff packet to Codex/Kimi without UI paste as the authority boundary.
Fork readiness/preflight command that reports missing identity, carrier projection, binding locus, submit strategy, related Site root, and task authority before launch.
Site-local recipient/role resolution for builder identities that works across User Site, PC Site, and client Sites without relying on global aliases only.
Post-launch verification that the child agent adopted the declared identity and target Site contract before it can claim tasks.
proposal: Add a narada operator-surface agent fork or narada agent start command as the canonical front door for architect-to-builder spawning.
Make dry-run the default posture and require explicit --exec for process launch.
Emit durable fork_handoff and fork_adoption evidence.
Support task-backed launch: from task number or work package, generate the builder prompt and acceptance criteria automatically.
Fail closed when identity, Site, runtime locus, or submit strategy is ambiguous.
payload.summary: Local User Site has pieces for agent instantiation, fork handoff packets, Windows Terminal carrier materialization, identity binding, and operator-surface input, but no single governed command that launches/adopts a builder from an architect work package.
payload.title: Operator-surface agent forking should have a canonical orchestration path
payload.why_now: CPY architect needs to delegate a clear implementation task to builder; doing this manually would repeat known drift risks around identity, context inheritance, and operator-surface binding.
